w:document.mc:Ignorable: w14 w15 w16se w16cid w16 w16cex w16sdtdh w16sdtfl w16du wp14

w:r.w:t: DESIGN AND IMPLEMENTATION OF AN ONLINE COURSE MARKETPLACE
Design and Implementation of an Online Course Marketplace
project is my own research under the guidance of
. The results stated are honest, my own work, not copied by any other work. All references
ncluding images, tables, figures, and citations—are clearly and fully sourced in the bibliography. I take full responsibility for even one copy that violates the school's statutes.
I would like to sincerely thank Dr.
enthusiasm to guide me throughout the process of making the design project to achieve today's results!
I would like to express my heartfelt gratitude to you for the invaluable guidance and support that you provided to us throughout the completion of the project.
consistency and depth of knowledge, coupled with your enthusiastic and dedicated spirit, helped me overcome challenges and develop skills comprehensively.

I also want to convey my appreciation for your precious assistance and advice in editing and refining the project. Your constructive feedback and detailed comments have aided me in gaining a deeper understanding of the content and enhancing my abilities in evaluation and self-assessment.
Additionally, I want to extend my thanks to the entire school, friends, family, and everyone who shared knowledge, opinions, and encouragement during the process of working on this project.

The support from you and the surrounding community has been a significant source of motivation, contributing greatly to the successful completion of this project. I believe that the knowledge and skills acquired here will serve as a solid foundation for future development.
Objectives and scope of the topic

In an era defined by digital transformation, education stands at a critical crossroads. The traditional boundaries of learning have dissolved, replaced by interconnected virtual environments that transcend physical limitations of time and space. My journey into developing an
emerges from this evolving educational landscape—one where technology and pedagogy intertwine to create new possibilities for knowledge acquisition and skill development.

This thesis represents more than an academic requirement; it embodies my vision for accessible, engaging, and effective online education. As we navigate a post-pandemic world where remote learning has shifted from contingency to mainstream practice, the need for thoughtfully designed e-learning experiences has never been more apparent. The digital divide continues to challenge educational equity, while advances in educational technology offer unprecedented opportunities to personalize learning pathways.

The following pages document the technical architecture and user experience design of the proposed online learning platform. The work is a combination of computer science, education and user experience design—a multidisciplinary approach that reflects the complex nature of modern educational technology.

As we continue to reimagine education for the digital age, I offer this contribution with the hope that it advances our collective understanding of effective e-learning design and implementation. The future of education is being written in code and connectivity, and I am honored to add my voice to this transformative conversation.
Objectives and scope of the topic
s to identify best practices and areas for improvement
that enhances the learning experience through intuitive navigation and engaging interface
including course management, user authentication, content delivery, and progress tracking systems
that can personalize educational content based on individual learning patterns
that ensures accessibility across various devices and screen sizes
and technical decisions to provide insights for similar projects
of e-learning design principles and user experience in educational technology
Security measures for protecting user data and educational content
User registration and profile management system
Course creation, enrollment, and management functionalities
Assessment tools including quizzes, assignments, and progress tracking
Administrative dashboard with analytics and reporting capabilities
Literature review on e-learning theories and best practices
User research through surveys and interviews to identify requirements
Usability testing to evaluate the platform's effectiveness
The system is clearly divided into two main components with detailed implementation specifications:
: Vite.js for faster development and optimized builds
: For component props typing and shared type definitions with backend
Component Separation: Clear distinction between UI, feature, and layout components
TypeScript Integration: Strong typing throughout components
Reusable UI Components: Well-designed Button, Alert, Spinner components
Hook Integration: Good use of Redux hooks and custom hooks
Responsive Design: Tailwind Classes for Mobile-Friendly Design
Custom configuration extending the base theme
Defined color palette, typography, spacing, and breakpoints
Dark mode support using Tailwind's dark mode strategy
Responsive variants for all screen sizes (mobile-first approach)
Protected route system for authenticated sections
Path-based code splitting for optimized loading
JWT token management with refresh token rotation
Role-based access control (buyer vs seller permissions)
Clear separation between controllers, services, repositories, and entities
RESTful API design with proper resource naming
Standard HTTP methods and status codes
Query parameter support for filtering, sorting, and pagination
Appropriate programming strategies for optimal query
JWT implementation with asymmetric key encryption
Unit tests for services and utilities
In today's rapidly evolving digital landscape,
s have become indispensable tools for educational innovation. My TypeScript-based

represents a strategic fusion of modern web development and educational technology. TypeScript offers crucial advantages through its static typing system, catching errors during development rather than at runtime—essential for applications where reliability directly impacts learning experiences. The platform addresses several critical educational needs: it promotes accessibility by transcending geographical limitations; enables interactive learning through dynamic content and real-time feedback; facilitates personalized learning pathways through adaptive algorithms; ensures scalability to handle varying user loads while maintaining performance; and leverages data analytics to provide instructors with valuable insights into learner engagement and content effectiveness. This graduation project demonstrates not only technical proficiency but also an understanding of educational challenges in the digital age, potentially transforming how knowledge is shared and accessed within academic communities and beyond.

incorporates a rich array of features designed to enhance the educational experience. At its core, the platform offers robust course management capabilities, allowing instructors to create, organize, and deliver varied content types including videos, readings, and interactive elements—all benefiting from TypeScript's type safety for consistent data handling. Student engagement is fostered through personalized learning paths that adapt based on performance metrics and learning preferences, implementing TypeScript interfaces to ensure standardized progress tracking.
Interactive learning tools include theory lessons, exercises and quizzes, and AI interactions

. Administrative features benefit from TypeScript's type checking to ensure data integrity across user management, enrollment systems, and analytics dashboards that provide actionable insights into learning patterns.

Mobile responsiveness and accessibility compliance round out the feature set, creating a comprehensive learning environment that reflects both technical proficiency and an understanding of modern educational needs.

architecture employs a modern, scalable approach optimized for educational applications. The frontend implements a component-based structure using React with TypeScript, ensuring type safety across the UI layer while enabling responsive design that adapts seamlessly to various devices. State management leverages TypeScript's union types and interfaces to maintain predictable application state with Redux or Context API. For the backend, I've adopted a microservices

architecture where discrete services handle specific functionalities like authentication, course management, and assessment processing—each benefiting from TypeScript's strong typing for API contracts. Data persistence utilizes a hybrid approach with relational databases for structured user and course data, document stores for unstructured learning materials, and caching mechanisms for performance optimization. The communication layer employs RESTful APIs with comprehensive TypeScript interfaces defining request and response types, ensuring reliable data exchange between frontend and backend components. Security is implemented through JWT-based authentication, role-based access control, and data encryption—all enhanced by TypeScript's ability to enforce security patterns at compile time.

The TypeScript implementation strategy centers on leveraging the language's robust type system to enhance code quality and developer productivity. I start with a comprehensive type definition layer that models the educational domain—creating interfaces for courses, lessons, assessments, and user profiles with precise property typing to ensure data consistency throughout the application. The implementation employs generics extensively to create reusable components like paginated lists, form handlers, and data fetching utilities that work across different entity types while maintaining type safety. For API communication, I define explicit request and response interfaces using TypeScript's utility types, enabling compile-time validation before data leaves or enters the application. Error handling benefits from TypeScript's union types to represent different error states with associated data. Code organization follows a feature-based modular approach with clear separation of concerns, where shared types are centralized in a dedicated types directory. This comprehensive TypeScript strategy ensures a maintainable codebase that can evolve alongside educational requirements while minimizing runtime errors that could disrupt the learning experience.

focuses on interoperability with existing educational ecosystems. The platform supports data exchange through standardized formats, with TypeScript interfaces ensuring consistency during import and export operations. The platform is containerized using
, with type-safe configuration management across different environments
TypeScript helping to ensure that logging and metrics collection are consistent and reliable. This comprehensive approach to integration and deployment ensures that the
can operate effectively within broader educational technology ecosystems while maintaining the reliability and performance necessary for effective learning experiences.

has significant potential to transform educational experiences. The combination of type safety and flexible educational features enables instructors to focus on pedagogical innovation rather than worrying about system reliability. Students benefit from a consistent, personalized learning environment that adapts to their needs while providing immediate feedback—strengthening the learning process through continuous engagement. As educational technology continues to evolve, the platform's TypeScript foundation provides adaptability to incorporate emerging trends such as AI-powered tutoring, advanced learning analytics, and immersive educational experiences. Future development could expand the platform's capabilities to include machine learning models for predicting student success, blockchain-based credential verification, or extended reality components for experiential learning—all made more reliable through TypeScript's strong typing system. The platform demonstrates how thoughtful technical choices can enhance educational outcomes, creating a foundation for continuous improvement in how knowledge is shared, accessed, and applied.
This comprehensive approach to developing a TypeScript-based

represents a significant contribution to educational technology. By leveraging TypeScript's powerful type system throughout the application—from frontend components to backend services and API contracts—the platform achieves a level of reliability and maintainability crucial for educational applications. The thoughtful feature set addresses core needs in modern education, while the scalable architecture ensures the platform can grow alongside educational demands. This graduation project exemplifies how modern web development practices can be applied to create meaningful solutions that enhance learning experiences. As education continues its digital transformation, platforms like this will play an increasingly important role in making knowledge more accessible, engaging, and effective for diverse learners worldwide.

is a modern React application built entirely in Typescript, applying basic to advance techniques and best practices in web application development. The architecture is designed to support multiple user roles (administrators, teachers and students) with separate interfaces and functions for each group
In addition, the User Interface is enhanced when using

thanks to its high customizability and optimized performance. This CSS framework allows for rapid development through utility classes that help design responsively for many different devices. The system uses libraries such as React Query for state management and API interaction, Redux for global state management, and React Router for smooth navigation between pages. The front-end architecture is organized in a component-based model, with components that are small, highly reusable, and easy to maintain. Custom hooks are developed to handle complex logic such as user authentication, authorization, and online learning session management. To enhance the learning experience, the system integrates interactive features such as real-time chat, bulletin boards, and intuitive learning progress tracking tools.

wp:docPr.name: Picture 1
wp:docPr.descr: Typescript là gì? Vì sao TypeScript là lựa chọn hàng đầu cho Dự án lớn | 200Lab Blog

pic:cNvPr.name: Picture 1
pic:cNvPr.descr: Typescript là gì? Vì sao TypeScript là lựa chọn hàng đầu cho Dự án lớn | 200Lab Blog

w:r.w:t: TypeScript is a project source code developed by
it can be considered as an upgrade of
because the version adds static typing options and class-driven objects which are not present in
. TypeScript can be used to develop applications that run on the client side (Angular2) and server side (NodeJS) [1].

TypeScript provides significant benefits for modern web development through its static type checking system, which catches errors during development rather than runtime, dramatically reducing debugging time and preventing production bugs. The enhanced IDE support offers powerful features like intelligent auto-completion, real-time error highlighting, and seamless code navigation, creating a more productive development experience.

The language serves as self-documenting code through type definitions that clearly communicate data structures and component interactions, making it invaluable for team collaboration and code maintenance. During refactoring, TypeScript's comprehensive type checking acts as a safety net, immediately flagging compatibility issues before they become runtime errors—especially crucial for large applications.
TypeScript enables developers to use modern JavaScript features while maintaining compatibility across different runtime environments through configurable

. The language extends JavaScript with robust object-oriented programming constructs including interfaces, generics and access modifiers, facilitating cleaner architecture and better code organization for complex applications like
The growing ecosystem further strengthens TypeScript's value proposition, with thousands of type definitions available through
and increasing native TypeScript support from major libraries and frameworks, ensuring seamless integration with existing tools and reducing development friction.

TypeScript presents several challenges despite its benefits. The primary obstacle is the learning curve, as developers must master new concepts like interfaces, generics, and type guards, temporarily slowing development during adoption. The additional build step adds complexity, requiring transpilation and build tool configuration while increasing compilation times. Writing comprehensive type definitions creates ongoing overhead, especially for complex objects or libraries lacking proper types. TypeScript can provide false security since type assertions can bypass checking and runtime errors remain possible at code boundaries. Integration with existing JavaScript libraries often requires creating separate type declarations, adding maintenance

burden. Initial project setup involves more complexity than JavaScript, requiring configuration of compilers, build tools, and testing frameworks. Advanced features can also increase bundle sizes if not properly optimized. However, for complex applications like
s, the long-term benefits of improved maintainability and fewer runtime errors typically justify the initial investment costs.

wp:docPr.name: Picture 2
wp:docPr.descr: The Power of Tailwind CSS: Simplify Your Styling Workflow | by sahil shah | Medium

pic:cNvPr.name: Picture 3
pic:cNvPr.descr: The Power of Tailwind CSS: Simplify Your Styling Workflow | by sahil shah | Medium

w:r.w:t: Nowadays, when creating a new website in a short period of time or with a small development team, most people tend to use Bootstrap Framework to help speed up the development process. Bootstrap’s ease of use, rich documentation, and many predefined CSS components are the reasons why Bootstrap is so popular. However, as the project gets bigger and more users start using it, developer performance and page load concerns arise. While using Bootstrap, it is likely that all the available utility classes will be used, leading to a significant increase in CSS as well as unused components. One solution is to use Tailwind CSS Framework.
Tailwind is a CSS Framework for Rapid UI Development. When it comes to CSS Frameworks, the first things that come to mind are Bootstrap, Material Design or

. However, although Tailwind is considered a CSS Framework, it does not follow the same philosophy as other CSS Frameworks. Tailwind is primarily a widget framework or rather a widget package. Tailwind does not provide a default theme or any predefined UI components.

Tailwind provides tools for rapid development while allowing the developer to maintain. While there is no limit to the imagination with Tailwind, the design is structured in such a way that the developer can create a self-imposed rule to avoid increasing the size of CSS or repeating properties.

Tailwind CSS provides exceptional flexibility through its utility-first approach, offering granular control over design elements without fighting against opinionated framework styles. This methodology accelerates development by eliminating custom CSS writing, allowing developers to rapidly prototype and iterate by applying utility classes directly in HTML without context-switching between files.
The framework produces highly optimized production builds through
integration, automatically removing unused utilities to create remarkably small file sizes that improve load times and application performance—crucial for

s where performance impacts user experience. Tailwind enforces consistent design systems through configurable design tokens controlling colors, spacing, and typography, preventing inconsistent UI elements and ensuring visual harmony across complex multi-role applications serving administrators, teachers, and students.

Responsive design becomes straightforward with Tailwind's intuitive breakpoint system, using prefix-based utilities to specify different styles across screen sizes without complex media queries, ensuring optimal experiences across all devices. The component extraction pattern encourages thoughtful reusability, allowing developers to start with utilities and extract components as patterns emerge, leading to organic component library evolution based on actual needs rather than assumptions. The growing ecosystem provides substantial advantages through numerous plugins, comprehensive documentation, and strong community support that accelerates problem-solving for complex development projects.

Tailwind CSS presents several challenges despite its benefits. The steep learning curve requires developers to memorize hundreds of utility classes, temporarily slowing productivity and potentially delaying team onboarding for e-learning projects. Many developers criticize Tailwind's approach of mixing presentation logic directly into HTML markup, arguing this violates separation of concerns principles and creates maintenance challenges in larger applications.

Tailwind projects often suffer from verbose HTML markup due to accumulated utility classes, making templates harder to read and maintain, especially for complex components. This density can impede code reviews and increase cognitive load when understanding component structures. Communication with design teams becomes challenging since designers think in component-level terms rather than individual utility properties, requiring additional documentation to bridge this conceptual gap.

Development environments include the entire utility library, resulting in large CSS files that can slow browser rendering and developer tools, particularly impacting less powerful machines as applications grow in complexity. Customizing Tailwind beyond defaults
requires additional setup and configuration knowledge, potentially presenting challenges when implementing specialized e-learning features.

Adopting Tailwind in existing projects presents migration challenges, as it represents a fundamentally different styling approach than traditional CSS methodologies. Incremental introduction requires careful planning to prevent style conflicts and maintain consistency. For university students developing

s, while Tailwind offers compelling advantages in development speed and design consistency, the learning curve and maintenance challenges should be carefully considered, especially for team projects with varying experience

wp:docPr.name: Picture 3
wp:docPr.descr: Vite là gì? Cách chạy ứng dụng Vite dễ dàng | TECHIE.VN

pic:cNvPr.name: Picture 5
pic:cNvPr.descr: Vite là gì? Cách chạy ứng dụng Vite dễ dàng | TECHIE.VN

w:r.w:t: Vite is a modern web application development and building tool, created by Evan You, the developer of Vue.js. Vite's goal is to solve the problems of speed, performance, and programming experience when developing web applications with React, Vue, and many other frameworks.

Meanwhile, React is a popular JavaScript library for building powerful and flexible user interfaces. When combining Vite and React (Vite React), you can take advantage of both technologies to create fast, smooth, and easily maintainable applications.

Vite.js delivers exceptional development speed through its innovative approach to module bundling, leveraging native ES modules to serve files on-demand rather than processing entire applications upfront. This architecture enables near-instantaneous server start times and lightning-fast hot module replacement (HMR), dramatically improving development experience for large applications like

The intelligent HMR system precisely updates only affected modules while preserving application state, allowing developers to see changes immediately without losing context or requiring full page reloads. This responsive feedback loop accelerates UI development and feature testing across complex interfaces. Vite provides seamless TypeScript integration out-of-the-box through
than traditional compilers without requiring complex configuration

The build optimization process generates production-ready assets with impressive efficiency through modern code splitting, tree shaking, and asset optimization, creating highly optimized bundles that improve load times and runtime performance—crucial for e-learning applications
performance impacts user experience and learning effectiveness.

Vite offers exceptional flexibility through its plugin ecosystem and framework-agnostic architecture, providing first-class support for React, Vue, and Svelte while remaining independent of specific UI libraries. The simplified configuration experience features sensible defaults requiring minimal adjustment, with a clear API for customization when needed. Built-in features include CSS preprocessing, static asset handling, and environment variable management, streamlining the development toolchain and allowing student developers to focus on application features rather than build tool maintenance.

Despite its strengths, Vite.js presents several challenges for development teams. The framework's relative newness means the ecosystem is still maturing, with some specialized plugins and integrations being unavailable or less robust than established bundlers like Webpack. This limitation might impact integration with specialized e-learning tools or libraries

Vite's development server relies heavily on native ES modules, creating compatibility challenges with older browsers during development. While production builds resolve most issues, testing in environments without modern JavaScript support may require additional configuration, affecting workflows when older browser support is required.

Migration from existing build tools presents complexities, as configurations, plugins, and optimizations may not directly translate between bundlers, requiring significant refactoring. For established

s with complex build processes, this overhead could temporarily impact development velocity. Documentation gaps regarding advanced configurations and edge cases can increase problem-solving time when implementing specialized requirements.

Vite's opinionated defaults occasionally conflict with specific project requirements or established patterns. While the configuration API allows overrides, some architectural decisions may require workarounds. For large-scale monorepo setups or non-standard project structures, Vite's conventions may prove restrictive, potentially impacting complex e-learning ecosystems with multiple interconnected applications.

Integration challenges arise with legacy code or older libraries relying on CommonJS modules or global dependencies. While compatibility plugins exist, these sometimes introduce complexity or performance penalties. For university students developing

s, Vite offers compelling advantages through development speed and simplified workflows, but ecosystem immaturity and compatibility challenges should be considered for projects with specific browser support or integration requirements.

wp:docPr.name: Hình ảnh 7
wp:docPr.descr: How To Use the MongoDB Shell | DigitalOcean

pic:cNvPr.name: Picture 11
pic:cNvPr.descr: How To Use the MongoDB Shell | DigitalOcean

w:r.w:t: MongoDB is a NoSQL document-oriented database management system designed to store, retrieve, and manage document-based data. Unlike traditional relational databases that use tables and rows, MongoDB stores data as BSON (Binary JSON) documents within collections. This allows MongoDB to handle complex and flexible data structures naturally.

MongoDB was developed by 10gen (now MongoDB Inc.) in 2007 and has become one of the world's most popular NoSQL databases. The name "MongoDB" derives from the word "humongous," reflecting its capability to handle massive volumes of data

MongoDB is a highly popular NoSQL database known for its flexibility, scalability, and performance. One of its primary advantages is its schema-less design, which allows developers to store documents with varying structures. This makes MongoDB ideal for applications where requirements evolve over time. Its document-based storage format (BSON) is similar to JSON, making it intuitive and easy to work with, especially for developers familiar with JavaScript and modern web technologies. MongoDB also offers high performance through fast read and write operations, and its ability to index data ensures low latency access.

Another significant benefit of MongoDB is its support for horizontal scalability through sharding, which enables the system to handle large volumes of data and traffic by distributing data across multiple servers. It also ensures high availability via replica sets, allowing automatic failover and redundancy to minimize downtime. The database supports a powerful query language that includes features like filtering, aggregation, full-text search, and geospatial queries, along with robust indexing capabilities.

MongoDB is well-suited for modern applications such as real-time analytics, content management systems, mobile apps, and IoT solutions. Additionally, it includes built-in security features such as authentication, role-based access control, and encryption options, which help protect sensitive data. Overall, MongoDB provides a flexible, performant, and developer-friendly platform for building scalable and reliable applications.

Despite its many strengths, MongoDB also has some limitations that may make it less suitable for certain applications. One major drawback is the lack of support for complex joins and transactions in earlier versions, which can make it harder to maintain data integrity in systems that rely heavily on relational data. Although newer versions have added multi-document transaction support, it still doesn't match the robustness and maturity of traditional relational databases like PostgreSQL or MySQL. Additionally, because MongoDB uses a flexible schema, it can lead to inconsistent data if not carefully managed, especially in large teams or projects without strict data validation. Storage can also be less efficient, as documents often store redundant data and the BSON format can consume more space than equivalent
relational tables. Lastly, while MongoDB provides great scalability, managing sharded clusters and replicas can add complexity and require more operational effort.

data store that operates entirely in-memory. Developed by Salvatore Sanfilippo in 2009, Redis has become one of the most popular NoSQL databases today, featuring extremely fast speeds and low latency, making it a great choice for many real-time applications and scenarios

Redis is extremely fast since it's an in-memory database - data access happens at RAM speed rather than disk speed. This makes it perfect for caching, session storage, and real-time applications where millisecond response times matter

It supports rich data structures beyond simple key-value pairs, including lists, sets, sorted sets, hashes, and streams. This flexibility lets you model complex data relationships efficiently. Redis also provides built-in persistence options, so you can save your in-memory data to disk periodically or log every write operation.

The pub/sub messaging feature makes Redis useful for real-time notifications and chat applications. It handles high concurrency well and can process hundreds of thousands of operations per second. Redis also supports clustering and replication for scaling across multiple servers.
Atomic operations ensure data consistency even with concurrent access, and features like expiration policies automatically clean up old data, making it ideal for caching scenarios.

The biggest limitation is that your dataset must fit in RAM, which can be expensive for large amounts of data. If your server crashes and you haven't configured persistence properly, you could lose data since everything is stored in memory.
Redis has limited query capabilities compared to SQL databases - you can't perform complex joins or analytical queries. It's designed for simple operations on individual keys or data structures.

Memory management can be tricky - Redis doesn't automatically free memory when you delete keys, and memory fragmentation can become an issue over time. The single-threaded nature (for data operations) means CPU-intensive operations can block other requests

Persistence options come with trade-offs: frequent snapshots impact performance, while append-only files can grow very large. Redis also lacks the ACID properties that traditional databases provide, so it's not suitable as a primary database for complex transactional applications.
This is a Use Case Diagram that illustrates the functions that an Admin can perform in my
system. This diagram clearly shows the role and permissions of the
in the system, demonstrating that the
has full authority to manage users, approve requests, and monitor platform activities. This is an important part of designing the authorization system for the
can effectively control and manage the entire system.
Monitor overall platform health and activity
Admin controls instructor accounts and permissions
Admin restricts or restores user access
Admin reviews and approves pending applications
Quality control before content goes live
Make data-driven decisions for platform improvement
This is a Use Case Diagram that illustrates the functions that a Teacher (Instructor) can perform in
system. This diagram demonstrates the comprehensive role of instructors in the

, showing they have full control over their educational content and student management. The Teacher role focuses on content creation, course management, and monitoring student progress, which are essential functions for delivering quality online education.
Teacher logs in or creates account
Teacher views list of students enrolled in their courses
This is a Use Case Diagram that illustrates the functions that a
system. This diagram showcases the student-centric learning experience in the
, emphasizing the learner's journey from discovery to completion. The
role focuses on content consumption, learning engagement, and accessing support features.
Student logs in or creates new account
Access learning platform and personal dashboard
Student explores available courses and content
Discover learning opportunities and course details
Gain access to course materials and community
Student views video lectures and materials
Learn course content and acquire knowledge
Purchase access to paid educational content
This is a Sequence Diagram that illustrates the user registration process flow in
User - The person registering for an account
Auth Service - Authentication service for security operations
User submits registration form with personal details
API validates email uniqueness in database
API sends password for secure hashing
API stores complete user profile in database
API returns success message with access token
This is a Sequence Diagram that illustrates the user login authentication process flow in my
User - The person attempting to log in
Auth Service - Authentication service for security operations
User submits email and password credentials
API queries database for user account
Database returns user profile and hashed password
API sends credentials for password validation
API requests access and refresh token creation
API returns tokens for authenticated session
This is a Sequence Diagram that illustrates the course creation process flow in
Instructor - The teacher creating a new course
Database - MongoDB database for data persistence
his sequence diagram represents a sophisticated course creation workflow with advanced file handling capabilities.
This diagram demonstrates an enterprise-grade content management system that handles complex multimedia course creation with robust file management, security, and performance optimization.
Instructor submits initial course details and metadata
API stores basic course information in database
Instructor uploads media files (videos, images, documents)
API saves uploaded files to storage system
Database returns accessible URLs for stored files
API updates course record with file references
API confirms successful course creation to instructor
This is a Sequence Diagram that illustrates the
payment and course enrollment process flow in my
Student - The learner purchasing a course
Database - MongoDB database for enrollment tracking
Student initiates course enrollment with course ID
returns payment QR code and transaction ID
Display QR code for mobile payment scanning
Student scans QR code and completes payment
Add student to course enrollment records
User submits search query with text and filters
Middleware checks for existing cached search results
Cache miss - no results found for this query
Forward search request to API for processing
API validates and processes search parameters
Execute full-text search with filters on course collection
Request accessible URLs for course thumbnails
Provide URLs for course media assets
Store search results in Redis for future requests
Return formatted search results to user
This is a Sequence Diagram that illustrates the comprehensive course search and caching architecture in your
User - The student searching for courses
Cache Middleware - Redis caching layer interceptor
Redis Cache - In-memory data store for performance
Cloud Service - AWS S3/CloudFront for media assets
Student initiates enrollment with course ID and payment info
System validates JWT token and student status
Student cleared to proceed with enrollment
Retrieve course information, pricing, and availability
API determines if payment processing is needed
Add student to course enrollment list
Increment enrollment count and update records
This is a Sequence Diagram that illustrates the complete course and lesson content management lifecycle in your
Teacher - The instructor creating and managing educational content
Database - MongoDB for course and lesson data
Media Storage - AWS S3/CloudFront for multimedia assets

This Admin User Management Sequence Diagram demonstrates a sophisticated platform governance system with comprehensive user lifecycle management. This sequence diagram represents a professional-grade administrative system that balances platform control with user rights, ensuring educational quality while maintaining transparent and accountable governance processes essential for a trusted e-learning environment.
Admin requests to see all users/instructors
API queries database for user information
Database returns user data with status
API presents formatted user list to admin
API changes instructor status to approved
API sets user status to blocked
API removes blocked status from user
This is a Sequence Diagram that illustrates the password reset functionality in your

system. This sequence demonstrates a secure and user-friendly password reset system that follows industry best practices for account recovery while maintaining the high security standards required for an
handling sensitive user data and learning content.
User clicks password reset link from email
API checks if reset token exists and is valid
Confirm token is valid and not expired
Display password reset form to user
Save new hashed password to user record
comprehensive online learning platform that connects teachers and students, allowing teachers to upload courses and students to access and learn from them.
Technology Stack: TypeScript, JavaScript, React.js, Node.js, Express.js, MongoDB, Redis, Docker, Clean Architecture
FR-001: The system must allow students to register accounts with email, password, full name, phone number, and interests
FR-002: The system must support Google OAuth login for students
FR-003: The system must allow instructors to register with detailed information including: certificates, qualifications, experience, skills
FR-004: The system must have separate login functionality for Admin
FR-005: The system must use JWT tokens for authentication and authorization
FR-006: The system must have automatic refresh token functionality
FR-007: Students can update personal information, profile picture, and change password
FR-008: Instructors can update profile, profile picture, and change password
The system must store profile pictures on cloud storage (AWS S3/
FR-010: Admin can view list of instructor registration requests
FR-011: Admin can accept or reject instructor registration requests
FR-012: The system must send email notifications for approval results
FR-013: Admin can block/unblock instructor accounts with specific reasons
FR-014: Admin can view list of all instructors and blocked instructors
FR-015: The system must maintain block/unblock history with reasons
FR-016: Admin can block/unblock student accounts with specific reasons
FR-017: Admin can view list of all students and blocked students
FR-018: Instructors can view list of students in their courses
FR-019: Instructors can create new courses with information: title, description, category, level, price, thumbnail, introduction video
FR-020: Instructors can edit information of created courses
FR-021: The system must support upload and storage of multimedia files (video, images)
FR-022: Courses must have information: syllabus, requirements, tags, duration
FR-023: Instructors can add lessons to courses with videos and documents
FR-024: Instructors can edit lesson content
FR-025: The system must support video streaming for lessons
FR-026: Students can view list of all courses
FR-027: Students can search courses by keywords, category, level
FR-028: The system must display recommended and trending courses
FR-029: Students can enroll in courses (free/paid)
FR-030: Students can view enrolled courses
FR-031: Instructors can create quizzes for each lesson
FR-032: Quizzes must support multiple-choice questions with multiple options
FR-033: The system must store and display quiz results
FR-034: Students can create discussion topics in each lesson
FR-035: Students can reply and interact in discussions
FR-036: Students can edit and delete their own comments
FR-037: The system must display creation and update time for discussions
FR-038: The system must integrate AI chatbot for learning support
FR-039: AI chat must save conversation history per user
FR-040: The system must support AI chat according to course/lesson context
NFR-001: Page load time must not exceed 3 seconds
NFR-002: API response time must not exceed 500ms for regular requests
NFR-003: Video streaming must be smooth, supporting adaptive bitrate
NFR-004: The system must support at least 1000 concurrent users
NFR-005: All API endpoints must be protected by JWT authentication
NFR-006: Passwords must be hashed using
NFR-007: The system must prevent XSS, CSRF attacks
NFR-008: Use HTTPS for all communications
NFR-009: Sanitize all user inputs to prevent MongoDB injection
NFR-010: Implement rate limiting for APIs
NFR-011: The system must be containerized with Docker
NFR-012: Database must support horizontal scaling
NFR-013: Use Redis cache to optimize performance
NFR-014: Clean Architecture for easy maintenance and extension
NFR-016: The system must have a backup strategy for database
NFR-017: Comprehensive error handling and logging
NFR-018: Responsive frontend, supporting mobile devices
NFR-019: Cross-browser compatibility (Chrome, Firefox, Safari, Edge)
NFR-020: Standard RESTful API, easy to integrate
TR-005: Redis for caching and session management
TR-013: Redux/Redux Toolkit for state management
TR-017: React OAuth for Google authentication
UIR-001: Interface must be responsive on all devices
UIR-003: Consistent design system with Tailwind CSS
UIR-005: Loading states for all async operations
MR-004: Unit tests for critical functions
MR-006: End-to-end testing for user flows

employs a sophisticated NoSQL database architecture built on MongoDB with Redis caching to deliver optimal performance and scalability. The database design follows a document-oriented approach with 10+ carefully structured collections including Students, Instructors, Courses, Lessons, Quiz, Discussions, AI Chat, and Payments, all interconnected through

references to maintain data relationships. The implementation leverages Mongoose ODM for robust schema validation, type safety, and data modeling, while incorporating a Repository Pattern that abstracts data access operations and ensures clean separation between business logic and database operations. Advanced features include comprehensive indexing strategies for performance optimization, Redis caching for frequently accessed data, file storage integration with cloud services for multimedia content, and security measures such as input sanitization and password hashing. The database architecture supports the platform's complex requirements including multi-role authentication (students, instructors, admin), course enrollment with payment processing, interactive discussions, AI-powered chat assistance, and real-time video streaming, all while maintaining data integrity through strict validation rules and ensuring scalability through horizontal scaling capabilities and efficient connection pooling.
Course belongs to categories and has multiple lessons
Students can review courses and earn achievements
Payment system links students, courses and instructors

This is an API Documentation Interface showing the admin login endpoint configuration for my E-Learning system. This API documentation interface demonstrates a professional development approach with comprehensive endpoint documentation, security configuration, and interactive testing capabilities essential for maintaining and scaling my

This is an API Documentation Interface showing a course management endpoint for my system. This API documentation demonstrates a course management system with proper administrative oversight, ensuring educational content quality and platform integrity through systematic course approval workflows.
This is an API Documentation Interface showing the learner/student management endpoint for

E-Learning system. This API endpoint demonstrates comprehensive student management capabilities that enable administrators to effectively oversee the learning community, monitor platform usage, and maintain educational quality standards while ensuring proper data security and privacy protection.

This is an Expanded API Documentation Interface showing the comprehensive admin management endpoints for my E-Learning system. This comprehensive API documentation demonstrates a full-featured administrative system that provides complete platform control, content moderation, user management, and business intelligence capabilities essential for operating a professional
see when they land on the site. This is usually where the main information is located, including the dashboard, available courses, and important links to other parts of the site.
The website provides a search bar and filters so students can find the courses they like.
has not paid for the course,
will not be able to attend the course, except for free courses.
Students can also see the prices of previous courses and reviews, to decide if it is right for them.
If the student has not paid for the course, they will be redirected to the payment page.
Course Content: "Course helps you master basic syntax and logic of Python programming language"
Modal overlay - Blurs background to focus on popup
Clear pricing display - Shows promotional vs original price clearly
Urgency creation - Countdown timer creates sense of urgency
Dual action options - Allows user to confirm or cancel
application to enhance user experience, especially for students in Vietnam. This is the VNPAY payment method selection page with the following functions:
QR Code payment: Payment via QR code scanning
QR Code display: Shows QR code for scanning
Mobile app integration: Integration with mobile banking apps
Domestic card payment: Payment with domestic cards
Bank building icon: Banking institution symbol
International card payment: International payment cards
Credit card icon: Credit card symbol
Multi-payment gateway: Support for multiple payment methods
Security integration: Integration of security standards
Mobile responsive: Responsive design for mobile devices
After successfully paying for the course, the system will return the course page as "
can start participating in the course.
The website also integrates an AI assistant to help answer students' questions, providing additional necessary knowledge quickly and conveniently.
Answer learning questions clear and easy-to-understand explanations
Encourage critical thinking, promote active learning

AI integration creates a symbiotic learning ecosystem that empowers both students and educators. For students, AI offers 24/7 personalized support—answering questions, explaining concepts, generating practice materials, and adapting to individual learning styles. This ensures continuous, judgment-free learning

anytime. For educators, AI reduces workload by handling routine queries and supporting content creation, assessment generation, and data-driven insights. This allows teachers to focus on complex, high-impact instruction and refine their methods based on real learning patterns. The result is a self-improving cycle: better AI support leads to stronger student performance, which informs better teaching, enhancing outcomes across the educational community.
This interface provides a complete overview for instructors to manage courses, track performance, and interact with students.
Displays a list of all courses
Admins: Browse and control all content
This is the Course Creation Interface that allows Instructors to create new courses with all the necessary information.
After the course is created, the system will send a request to the
Admin Dashboard TutorTrek is designed as a command center with 4 main functional groups:
The line chart shows revenue patterns over months
This chart helps admin easily manage the profits that the website brings, and at the same time provides data to base on the website development plan later.
Admin can manage students by viewing information, activity status and has the right to block students if there is a problem or when necessary.
Admin can also view and manage courses that instructors have created, including adding, editing, and deleting those courses.
uickly Search a particular category, and
ave time when there are multiple categories
After a period of research and study, closely guided by the lecturer, the project named "
Design and Implementation of an Online Course Marketplace

was completed according to the original plan. During the development process, I encountered many obstacles while still studying the remaining subjects at school, but after all the efforts and hard work, I completed the system within the scheduled time frame and accumulated a lot of experience, especially in strengthening my skills in web design, system analysis as well as time management. This project also helped me have a more general view of the software industry in general and website development thinking in particular. I successfully implemented the main features as stated in the project requirements and improved my ability to detect and solve problems during the development process. However, there are still some limitations. Although I have been exposed to many different programming languages, I have not specialized in any language. In addition, the current version of the website is not yet fully equipped for real-world deployment. Some functions are still incomplete or not fully optimized, which may affect the user experience, and the system's data security is not yet strong. Looking forward, I plan to continue improving and refining the website to enhance its performance and usability. I also plan to add more features to bring the website closer to real-world application standards.

, "JSON Web Token (JWT)," RFC 7519, Internet Engineering Task Force (IETF), May 2015. [Online]. Available: https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc7519 [9] R. C. Martin, Clean Architecture: A Craftsman's Guide to Software Structure and Design. Boston, MA, USA: Prentice Hall, 2017.
[10] Amazon Web Services, "Amazon Simple Storage Service (S3) User Guide," AWS Documentation, 2024. [Online]. Available: https://docs.aws.amazon.com/s3/
